methods.description: Process for germinating pollen grains in vitro using a specific medium to study pollen viability and morphology.

steps.description: Pollen grains were collected as described previously and suspended in tomato pollen germination medium (TPGM) containing 10% sucrose, 0.01% KNO3, 0.01% H3BO3, 0.02% MgSO4, 0.06% Ca(NO3)2 and 20 mM MES (pH 7.0).
steps.searchable_summary: collect and prepare pollen grains in TPGM

steps.description: Spread pollen on TPGM solidified with 0.5% agarose in 7 cm diameter petri dishes and germinate at room temperature in the dark for 12 hrs.
steps.searchable_summary: spread and germinate pollen on agarose medium

steps.description: Examine pollen germination by a Zeiss Axiovert inverted microscope.

methods.description: Procedure for studying pollen germination in vivo by pollinating flowers and analyzing the germination process.

steps.description: Label flowers subject to pollination and remove anthers one day before anthesis. Cover with plastic bags to prevent cross contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare flowers for in vivo pollination

steps.description: Collect fresh pollen grains from donor plants and place on stigmas of receptor plants using a painting brush. Cover with bags immediately after pollination.
Remove pollinated pistils after 24 h, fix in 3:1 ethanol:acetic acid for 1 h at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: fix pollinated pistils in ethanol:acetic acid

steps.description: Transfer pistils to 8 M NaOH for softening overnight, wash 3 times in distilled water, and incubate in 0.1% aniline blue overnight in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: soften and stain pistils with aniline blue

steps.description: Destain pistils in distilled water for 1-3 h, squash between cover slip and slide with glycerol, and examine by Zeiss Axioplan 2 microscope with AMCA filter.

methods.description: Examination of dry and hydrated pollen grains using electron microscopy to study their morphology.

steps.description: Examine dry and hydrated pollen grains using a Hitachi 4500 Field Emission Scanning Electron Microscope (FESEM).
steps.searchable_summary: use FESEM to examine pollen grains

steps.description: For low temperature scanning electron microscopy (LTSEM) of frozen-hydrated specimens, remove pistils containing pollen from flowers, freeze in liquid nitrogen, and view by FESEM equipped with a BALTEC cryo-stage.

methods.description: Comparison of LePro1 sequence with other plant profilins and structural analysis using online tools to understand sequence similarity and structural differences.

steps.description: Carry out a blast search in the NCBI database using the deduced amino acid sequence of LePro1 as a query.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLAST search for LePro1 sequence

steps.description: Conduct phylogenic analysis to reveal major clusters of profilins.
Perform sequence alignment to identify sequence similarities and differences.
Conduct online analysis of 3-D structure using the Swiss Model.
